title: WorkflowV2
description: The rebar workflow DSL, schema_version "2". A coordinate-free, GitHub-Actions-style `steps:` document, git-tracked under .rebar/workflows/<name>.yaml. v2 EXTENDS v1 (every v1 leaf step is a valid v2 step): it adds DECLARATIVE control flow expressed as DATA — `branch` (conditional), `loop` (bounded iteration, mandatory max_iterations), and `map` (fan-out over a collection) — each carrying a NESTED `body`/`then`/`else` step list (recursive). v1 had only an acyclic `needs` DAG plus an `if:` skip-guard; v2 keeps those and the same authoring ergonomics. This schema is IMMUTABLE and version-pinned at a stable $id: once shipped it is never edited in place; a new DSL version ships as workflow.v3.schema.json with a read-time v2->v3 up-conversion shim. `additionalProperties:false` everywhere so an unknown key is a located error rather than a silent no-op (the Argo lesson). A step is EXACTLY ONE of: scripted (`uses:`), agentic (`prompt:`), or a control construct (`branch`/`loop`/`map`) — never a mix (oneOf). Reference integrity (frame-scoped), the expression allow-list, loop-frame legality (raw `needs` cycles still rejected; iteration is ONLY via a declared loop frame), and the `map` `as`-binding scope are enforced by the linter (rebar.llm.workflow.lint), not by this structural schema.

schema_version.description: DSL version, typed as a STRING (never a YAML float). For this schema it is exactly "2"; a value the running rebar does not recognize is a hard upgrade-rebar error (WS-B3). An older file (schema_version "1") is up-converted to "2" at read time by the migrate shim.

name.description: Stable human workflow id (lowercase, no spaces); the file stem under .rebar/workflows/.

model.description: Workflow-level default model for agent steps, e.g. `anthropic:claude-opus-4-8`. Per-step `model` overrides this (precedence step > workflow > config > env > default).

inputs.description: Declared workflow inputs, referenced as `${{ inputs.<name> }}`.

steps.description: The ordered step list at the top frame. Execution order WITHIN a frame is the topological order of `needs` edges, NOT array position; array order is for human readability only. A `branch`/`loop`/`map` step carries its own nested frame(s).

expression.description: A `${{ … }}` expression from the closed allow-list (linter-enforced): inputs.<name>, steps.<id>.outputs.<name>, secrets.<name>, and the frame-scoped bindings loop.<var> / map.<as>.

step.description: A single workflow step. Exactly one of `uses` (scripted), `prompt` (agentic), `branch` (conditional), `loop` (bounded iteration), or `map` (fan-out) is required (oneOf). A control construct carries its nested body; it takes no `with`/`output_schema`/`mode`/`model`.

id.description: Stable, unique (within its frame) step id; the anchor for `${{ steps.<id>.outputs.* }}` references and the loop/map iteration key.

type.description: Optional explicit discriminator; if present it must agree with the uses/prompt/branch/loop/map shape (linter-checked). Normally inferred.

uses.description: Scripted step: the registry name of a deterministic built-in (rebar.llm.workflow.steps).

prompt.description: Agentic step: the prompt id resolving to .rebar/prompts/<id>.md (git-canonical; WS-F).

needs.description: Upstream step ids (SAME frame) this step depends on; defines the per-frame execution DAG. A `needs` edge may not cross into a sibling/inner frame, and the per-frame graph must be acyclic (iteration is expressed ONLY by a `loop` frame, never a `needs` back-edge).

with.description: Inputs to a scripted/agent step: a map of name -> literal or `${{ ... }}` expression (allow-listed; the linter forbids raw ${{}} leaking into prompt/command bodies).

output_schema.description: Agent step: the JSON Schema name constraining the step's structured output (default review_result).

mode.description: Agent step output-finalization strategy (WS-D1). `findings` = review_result pipeline; `structured` = validate against output_schema; `text` = raw text.

model.description: Agent step: per-step model override, e.g. `anthropic:claude-opus-4-8` or `openai:gpt-4o` (highest precedence).

if.description: Optional skip-guard expression (the step runs only when it evaluates truthy). Distinct from `branch`: `if` skips ONE step in place; `branch` selects between alternative nested frames.

branch.description: Conditional construct (-> a BPMN exclusiveGateway): run the `then` frame when `when` is truthy, else the optional `else` frame. Each branch is its own nested frame (recursive step list).

when.description: The condition expression selecting `then` (truthy) vs `else`. May NOT reference a secret (control flow must not branch on a credential).

then.description: The frame run when `when` is truthy.

else.description: The frame run when `when` is falsy (optional; omit for a one-armed guard).

loop.description: Bounded iteration construct (-> a BPMN standardLoopCharacteristics). The body re-executes per iteration; iteration is keyed (run_id, step_id, iteration). `max_iterations` is MANDATORY (the runaway guard). An optional `while`/`until` expression decides continuation (checked per iteration BEFORE running the body); with neither, the body runs exactly `max_iterations` times. The cycle is the loop construct ITSELF — the body's `needs` graph stays acyclic.

max_iterations.description: Hard upper bound on iterations (MANDATORY). Exceeding it is a runaway-loop error, never a silent stop.

while.description: Continue WHILE this is truthy (checked before each iteration). The loop condition MAY reference outputs of its own body steps (e.g. the previous iteration's score). Mutually exclusive with `until`.

until.description: Continue UNTIL this becomes truthy (i.e. stop when truthy). Mutually exclusive with `while`.

var.description: Name bound to the zero-based iteration index inside the body, referenced as `${{ loop.<var> }}` (default `index`).

map.description: Fan-out construct (-> a BPMN multiInstanceLoopCharacteristics): run the `body` frame once per element of the `over` collection, binding each element to `as`. Iterations are order-independent (iteration-keyed markers), so `max_concurrency` > 1 may run them concurrently (the bounded fan-out story) while replay stays deterministic.

over.description: An expression yielding the collection to fan out over (an input, an upstream step output, or an enclosing frame binding).

as.description: Name bound to the current element inside the body, referenced as `${{ map.<as> }}`.

index_var.description: Optional name bound to the zero-based element index inside the body, referenced as `${{ map.<index_var> }}`.

max_concurrency.description: Upper bound on concurrently-running iterations (default 1 = serial). Event commits are serialized regardless; only the agent calls run in parallel.
